perturbation.original_text: Voting Commitments. The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date (if the 2020 Annual Meeting is within thirty (30) days of the anniversary of the Company's 2019 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, such number of shares shall be no less than 90% of the Current Position) for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the right to vote the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties in their sole discretion with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting. The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: Voting Commitments. The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board. The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.explanation: By removing specific voting commitments related to stockholder proposals, ratification of the accounting firm, and say-on-pay, the obligation of the White Hat Parties is diminished. The omission of these specific obligations creates an in-text contradiction because it is implied elsewhere in the agreement that the White Hat Parties will vote in accordance with the Board's recommendations for these specific proposals, which are now removed from section 4. The reference to providing written evidence of votes remains, but the scope of what votes are covered is now unclear due to the lack of specifics.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Voting Commitments. The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board. The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes specific voting commitments outlined in the original text regarding stockholder proposals, ratification of the accounting firm, and say-on-pay. This creates a contradiction because, while the obligation to provide written evidence of votes remains, the scope of what votes are covered is now unclear due to the lack of specifics. The contradicted text, which is essentially a truncated version of the original, highlights this discrepancy. The omission in the changed text contradicts the implied obligation (within the original context) of voting in accordance with the Board's recommendations for these specific proposals, as this is no longer explicitly stated or required.

perturbation.original_text: Support Period Covenants. Except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, at all times during the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the date that is thirty (30) days prior to expiration of the advance notice period for the submission by stockholders of director nominations for consideration at the 2021 Annual Meeting as set forth in the Bylaws, the White Hat Parties shall not, and shall cause the White Hat Representatives not to, directly or indirectly, in any manner, alone or in concert with others: (a) attempt to call a special meeting of stockholders of the Company; or (b) make a request for any stockholder list or other Company books and records, whether pursuant to Section 220 of the Delaware General Corporation Law or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: Support Period Covenants. Except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, at all times during the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the date that is thirty (30) days prior to expiration of the advance notice period for the submission by stockholders of director nominations for consideration at the 2021 Annual Meeting as set forth in the Bylaws, the White Hat Parties shall not, and shall cause the White Hat Representatives not to, directly or indirectly, in any manner, alone or in concert with others: (a) attempt to call a special meeting of stockholders of the Company; or (b) make a request for any stockholder list or other Company books and records.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to "Section 220 of the Delaware General Corporation Law or otherwise" from the clause preventing the White Hat Parties from requesting stockholder lists or company records, it creates uncertainty. While the restriction on requesting such information remains, the absence of the specific legal reference implies that the White Hat Parties *may* be able to pursue these actions through channels *other* than Section 220, thus potentially contradicting the intent of the clause. It is now unclear if the intent was to broadly restrict these requests or only restrict them under that specific Delaware law. The contradiction stems from implying the restriction is not absolute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Support Period Covenants. Except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, at all times during the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the date that is thirty (30) days prior to expiration of the advance notice period for the submission by stockholders of director nominations for consideration at the 2021 Annual Meeting as set forth in the Bylaws, the White Hat Parties shall not, and shall cause the White Hat Representatives not to, directly or indirectly, in any manner, alone or in concert with others: (a) attempt to call a special meeting of stockholders of the Company; or (b) make a request for any stockholder list or other Company books and records.
perturbation.justification: The removal of "Section 220 of the Delaware General Corporation Law or otherwise" narrows the scope of the restriction on requesting stockholder lists and company records. The original text aims to prevent these requests altogether. By omitting "or otherwise," the changed text implies that requests through other legal avenues might be permissible, contradicting the apparent original intent of a blanket prohibition as presented in the Contradicted Text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it introduces uncertainty and potentially allows actions that the original clause appears to prevent.
